agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, cart, checkout, promotions, orders, and refunds lifecycle. The rules govern inventory reservation timing, promotional pricing constraints, order immutability post-payment, and refund eligibility windows.","bounded_context":"Order Management & Checkout — covering cart operations, inventory reservation, payment confirmation, promotional/discount application, and post-purchase refund policy.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient collection of items a customer intends to purchase. Holding items in the cart does NOT reserve inventory."},{"term":"Checkout","definition":"The process initiated when a customer commits to purchasing cart contents. Triggers inventory reservation and starts the payment window."},{"term":"Inventory Reservation","definition":"A time-bound hold on inventory units, created when checkout begins and released if payment is not completed within the reservation window."},{"term":"Reservation Window","definition":"The 15-minute period after checkout initiation during which inventory is held for the customer awaiting payment."},{"term":"Abandoned Checkout","definition":"A checkout session where payment was not confirmed within the reservation window, resulting in automatic release of reserved inventory."},{"term":"Active Promotion","definition":"A currently-effective promotional pricing rule applied to a product. A product may have at most one active promotion at any time."},{"term":"Discount Code","definition":"A customer-supplied code that applies a discount to an order. Mutually exclusive with promotional pricing — only one discount mechanism applies per order."},{"term":"Discount Mechanism","definition":"Any source of price reduction: either a discount code OR a product promotion. These are mutually exclusive per order."},{"term":"Payment Confirmed","definition":"The irreversible state transition indicating successful payment. After this point, the order is immutable."},{"term":"Order Immutability","definition":"The property that an order cannot be modified in any way after payment is confirmed."},{"term":"Zero Inventory","definition":"A product state where available stock is zero. The product may remain listed/visible but cannot be purchased."},{"term":"Refund Window","definition":"The 30-day period after delivery during which a customer may request a refund."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Adding items to the cart does not reserve inventory. Inventory is only reserved when the checkout process begins.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If inventory were reserved at cart-add, a malicious or forgetful user could lock up inventory indefinitely, corrupting stock availability for all other customers. The cart-vs-checkout reservation boundary is a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: After a customer adds a product to their cart, the product's available inventory count remains unchanged."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"If payment is not completed within 15 minutes of checkout initiation, the inventory reservation is automatically released.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 15-minute window is a business policy parameter. It could be adjusted (e.g., to 10 or 20 minutes) without corrupting system state. The principle of time-bound reservation is invariant; the specific duration is policy.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When 15 minutes elapse after checkout initiation without payment confirmation, all inventory reservations for that checkout session are released and available inventory is restored."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A product may have at most one active promotion at any given time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Multiple simultaneous promotions on a single product create ambiguity in which price to display and charge, leading to data integrity issues, pricing errors, and potential revenue loss. This is a structural constraint on the promotion model.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Any attempt to activate a second promotion on a product that already has an active promotion is rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Discount codes cannot be combined with promotional pricing. Only one discount mechanism (either a discount code or a promotional price) applies per order.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing stacking of discount mechanisms without explicit business authorization results in unintended compounding discounts, potential below-cost sales, and revenue leakage. The mutual exclusivity is a pricing integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a customer applies a discount code to an order containing a product with an active promotion, the system applies exactly one discount mechanism and prevents the other from being applied."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Orders cannot be modified after payment is confirmed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Post-payment modification would break financial reconciliation, fulfillment accuracy, and audit trails. Order immutability after payment is a fundamental data integrity and financial correctness constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Any attempt to modify order contents, quantities, or pricing after payment confirmation is rejected by the system."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A product with zero available inventory cannot be purchased, even if the product remains listed in the catalog.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Selling inventory that does not exist creates fulfillment obligations that cannot be met, leading to overselling, customer dissatisfaction, and operational chaos. This is a physical constraint — you cannot ship what you do not have.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a customer attempts to check out with a product that has zero available inventory, the checkout is rejected for that product."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refund requests must be submitted within 30 days of the delivery date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 30-day refund window is a business policy that could be extended or shortened by management decision (e.g., holiday extended returns). The principle of a bounded refund window is sound, but the specific duration is policy.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: A refund request submitted more than 30 days after the recorded delivery date is rejected."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has added a product with 5 units in stock to their cart","when":"The customer views the cart without initiating checkout","then":"The product's available inventory remains 5 and other customers can still purchase all 5 units"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer initiates checkout with a product that has 3 units available","when":"The checkout process begins and 1 unit is requested","then":"1 unit is reserved, available inventory decreases to 2 for other customers"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer initiated checkout 15 minutes ago and has not completed payment","when":"The reservation window expires","then":"All reserved inventory for that checkout session is released and available inventory is restored"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A customer initiated checkout 14 minutes ago and has not yet paid","when":"The customer submits payment at minute 14","then":"The payment is accepted and the reservation is honored because the window has not expired"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A product already has an active promotion offering 20% off","when":"An admin attempts to add a second promotion (buy-one-get-one) to the same product","then":"The system rejects the second promotion and the original 20% off promotion remains active"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer's cart contains a product with an active 15% promotional discount","when":"The customer applies a discount code for 10% off at checkout","then":"The system applies only one discount mechanism and clearly indicates which one is applied and that the other cannot be combined"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A customer has completed payment and the order status is 'payment confirmed'","when":"The customer or an admin attempts to modify the order (change quantity, add item, change address)","then":"The system rejects the modification and the order remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A product is listed in the catalog with zero available inventory","when":"A customer attempts to add it to their cart and proceed to checkout","then":"The system prevents the purchase and informs the customer the product is out of stock"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A customer received a delivery 29 days ago","when":"The customer submits a refund request","then":"The refund request is accepted for processing"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A customer received a delivery 31 days ago","when":"The customer submits a refund request","then":"The refund request is rejected with an explanation that the 30-day refund window has passed"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"Two customers each have 1 unit of a product (2 remaining) in their carts and neither has started checkout","when":"Both customers initiate checkout simultaneously for 1 unit each","then":"Both reservations succeed because 2 units are available; inventory is reserved for both"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"Two customers attempt to check out the last 1 unit of a product simultaneously","when":"Both checkout processes attempt to reserve the same unit","then":"Exactly one reservation succeeds and the other is rejected due to insufficient inventory"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer's checkout reservation expires at the exact moment they submit payment","expected_behavior":"System must handle the race condition atomically. Either the payment is accepted (reservation still valid) or rejected (reservation expired). No partial state is acceptable."},{"scenario":"Product inventory drops to zero due to another customer's reservation while current customer has it in their cart","expected_behavior":"When the current customer initiates checkout, the system rejects the checkout for that product since available inventory is zero. The cart may still show the item but checkout cannot proceed for it."},{"scenario":"An active promotion is deactivated while a customer is mid-checkout with the promotional price","expected_behavior":"The price locked at checkout initiation should be honored for the duration of the reservation window. Changing prices mid-checkout creates trust and legal issues."},{"scenario":"Customer applies a discount code to an order where some products are on promotion and others are not","expected_behavior":"The spec says one discount mechanism per order. Clarify whether the discount code applies only to non-promoted items, or if the entire order must use one mechanism uniformly. This is an ambiguity in the spec."},{"scenario":"Refund requested on exactly day 30 after delivery","expected_behavior":"The request should be accepted — 'within 30 days' includes day 30 (boundary is inclusive)."},{"scenario":"Delivery date is disputed — customer claims non-delivery but system shows delivered","expected_behavior":"The 30-day refund window is anchored to the recorded delivery date. Disputed deliveries are a separate process and should not be governed by the same 30-day rule."},{"scenario":"Customer abandons checkout at minute 14, then re-initiates checkout at minute 16","expected_behavior":"The original reservation was released at minute 15. The new checkout creates a fresh reservation, subject to current inventory availability."},{"scenario":"Product inventory reaches zero between cart-add and checkout initiation","expected_behavior":"Checkout is rejected for that product. Cart contents are not a guarantee of availability."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect inventory reservation logic can lead to overselling (fulfillment costs, refunds, reputation damage) or underselling (locked phantom inventory reducing sales). Discount stacking bugs can cause significant margin erosion. The 15-minute reservation window balances conversion optimization against inventory lock-up costs.","operational":"Reservation timeout automation is critical — manual release of abandoned checkouts would be operationally unsustainable at scale. Promotion uniqueness constraint simplifies pricing engine logic and reduces support tickets from price confusion. Order immutability post-payment simplifies fulfillment pipeline and audit compliance.","user":"Customers expect items visible in catalog to be purchasable — the zero-inventory-still-listed scenario will cause friction if not handled with clear messaging. The 15-minute payment window is tight for some payment methods (bank transfers). Discount mechanism exclusivity may frustrate customers expecting to stack deals.","risk":"Race conditions in inventory reservation under high concurrency (flash sales) are the highest technical risk. Timezone and clock-skew issues in the 30-day refund window calculation pose compliance risk. Ambiguity in how discount codes interact with partially-promoted orders creates pricing risk.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Directly affected by inventory availability at checkout, discount application rules, refund window enforcement, and order immutability. Clear communication of these rules at each touchpoint is essential."},{"group":"Product/Merchandising Team","impact":"Must manage promotions with the one-active-promotion-per-product constraint. Needs tooling to view and manage active promotions to avoid conflicts."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Order immutability post-payment simplifies reconciliation. Discount exclusivity rules prevent unplanned margin erosion. Refund window provides bounded liability."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will handle disputes around reservation timeouts, discount exclusivity frustrations, and refund window edge cases. Needs clear policies and system visibility."},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Depends on accurate inventory reservation to prevent overselling. Reservation release timing directly affects pick/pack planning."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Must implement atomic inventory reservation, handle concurrency in checkout, enforce promotion constraints, and build reliable timer-based reservation release."}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The spec is well-articulated with clear, non-contradictory rules. All seven business rules are explicitly stated and internally consistent. Confidence is not higher because there is one notable ambiguity around discount code behavior on orders with a mix of promoted and non-promoted products — the spec says 'per order' but doesn't clarify partial application. Additionally, the spec does not address the price-lock question during checkout (what happens if prices or promotions change mid-checkout).","high_confidence_areas":["Inventory reservation timing (cart vs checkout) is unambiguous","15-minute reservation timeout and release mechanism","One active promotion per product constraint","Order immutability after payment confirmation","Zero inventory prevents purchase","30-day refund window from delivery date"],"low_confidence_areas":["How discount exclusivity applies when an order contains a mix of promoted and non-promoted products — does the discount code apply to non-promoted items only, or is it all-or-nothing per order?","Whether the price/promotion active at checkout initiation is locked for the reservation window duration","Whether the zero-inventory check happens at cart-add, checkout initiation, or payment confirmation (spec implies checkout but cart-add blocking may also be desired)","Timezone and exact boundary semantics for the 30-day refund window"],"assumptions":["The 15-minute reservation window is a configurable policy parameter, not a hard-coded constant","'Within 30 days' is inclusive of day 30","Inventory reservation is atomic — no partial reservations","The system has a reliable mechanism (scheduler/cron) to release expired reservations","Discount mechanism priority (which one wins when both are present) is a business decision yet to be defined"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, cart, checkout, promotions, orders, and refunds lifecycle. The rules govern inventory reservation timing, promotional pricing constraints, order immutability post-payment, and refund eligibility windows.","bounded_context":"Order Management & Checkout — covering cart operations, inventory reservation, payment confirmation, promotional/discount application, and post-purchase refund policy.","ubiquitous_langu

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this e-commerce domain scenario carefully.

The rules are clear and well-defined. Let me classify each:

1. Inventory reserved at checkout, not cart add
2. 15-minute reservation timeout
3. Max one active promotion per product
4. Discount codes can't combine with promotional pricing
5. Orders immutable after payment confirmed
6. Zero inventory = can't purchase
7. Refunds within 30 days of delivery

No contradictions, no ambiguity, no missing context that's critical. These are straightforward e-commerce rules.


Now I'll work through the analysis and document the findings.
